subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Identify an equation as a statement of equality
Translate simple word phrases into algebraic expressions
Check if a given value is a solution to an equation
Understand the Left Hand Side (LHS) and Right Hand Side (RHS)

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Equations
topics_covered: The equality sign as a balance
teaching_strategy: Visualizing equality using a physical balance scale
student_activities: Write 'True' or 'False' for statements like $5 + 3 = 8$ and $x + 2 = 5$ (if $x=3$)
assessment: Oral quiz on LHS vs RHS

lesson_plan.title: Forming Equations
topics_covered: Writing equations from daily life scenarios
student_activities: Form an equation for: 'The age of Rahul is 5 years more than his sister'

teacher_tip: Stress that an equation is like a balanced scale; if the two sides aren't equal, it's not an equation!
